```{r}
	  list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[singProportionIdentifier]]$resultShapiroWilk_first100 = resultShapiroWilk_test[1:100]
	  
	  N <- length(list_of_networkRandomized)
		matrice_rewired <- 
			sapply(list_of_networkRandomized, function(elemento_lista) {
				elemento_lista$local_balance_index_rewired})
		nomi_osservati <- names(local_balance_index)
		p_values_raw <- sapply(nomi_osservati, empirical_pvalues_computation)
		list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[singProportionIdentifier]]$p_values_raw = p_values_raw[1:100]
		p_values_adjusted <- p.adjust(p_values_raw[1:100], method = "BH")
		p_values_adjusted_test = ifelse(p_values_adjusted < 0.05, 1, 0)
		list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[singProportionIdentifier]]$p_values_adjusted = p_values_adjusted
	  
	  desired_order <- names(local_balance_index)
		matrice_rewired <- matrice_rewired[desired_order, ]
		# identical(rownames(matrice_rewired), names(local_balance_index))
		
		plotTitle = paste0(chosen_percentage, "% edge keeped ", 
                   as.character(sign_proportions_specifier[singProportionIdentifier]), "-",
                   as.character(100-sign_proportions_specifier[singProportionIdentifier]),
                   " sign proportion")
		
		robust_z = robust_z_computation(local_balance_index, matrice_rewired)
		list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[singProportionIdentifier]]$robust_z = robust_z
	  list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[singProportionIdentifier]]$plotTitle = plotTitle
	  
	  if (elaborateGeneLists)
	  {
	    if (gene_from_qValues)
	    {
        genes_underThreshold_first100 = which(p_values_adjusted_test != 0)
        geneSelected_3 <- names(genes_underThreshold_first100)	      
	    }
	    if(gene_from_robustz)
	    {
        robust_z_testResult_2 = ifelse(robust_z <= -1.96, 1, 0)
        robust_z_testResult_2_first100 = robust_z_testResult_2[1:100]
    	  genes_underThreshold_first100 = which(robust_z_testResult_2_first100 != 0)
        geneSelected_3 <- names(genes_underThreshold_first100)	      
	    }
      
      geneName_converted_3 = convert_ensembl_to_names(geneSelected_3)
      correctOrder_3 <- match(geneSelected_3, geneName_converted_3$ensembl_gene_id)
      geneName_converted_3 <- geneName_converted_3[correctOrder_3, ]
      if (any(is.na(correctOrder_3))) {
        warning("Attenzione: alcuni geni nel vettore non sono stati trovati nel dataframe!")
      }
      
      
      list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[singProportionIdentifier]]$geneName_allIndexExclusive_first100 = geneName_converted_3

      
  	  if (saveGenes)
  	  {
    	  # robust_z_testResult = ifelse(robust_z >= 1.96 | robust_z <= -1.96, 1, 0)
        # sum(robust_z_testResult)
  
        
        fileTitle_3 = paste0("/", chosen_percentage, "perc_", 
                           as.character(sign_proportions_specifier[singProportionIdentifier]), "-",
                           as.character(100-sign_proportions_specifier[singProportionIdentifier]),
                           "_under2_InFirst100.xlsx")
        write.xlsx(geneName_converted_3, file = paste0(mainFolder, fileTitle_3))	    
          
  	    
  	  }      
	  }
	  


    

}




```

```{r}

loadData = TRUE
if(loadData)
{
  chosen_percentage = "10"

  list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy =
    readRDS(paste0(mainFolder,"/list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy_", chosen_percentage,  "%"))
}



# 1. Prepariamo la griglia 2x2
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
# mar = c(bottom, left, top, right)
par(mar = c(4.1, 4.1, 3.1, 2.1))


# 2. Iteriamo attraverso i 4 elementi della lista
for (i in 1:4) {
  
  # Estraiamo i dati correnti per chiarezza
  dati_correnti <- list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$robust_z
  titolo_corrente <- list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$plotTitle
  
  # Creiamo il plot seguendo le tue specifiche
  plot(dati_correnti, 
       xlab = "Genes", 
       ylab = "scores", 
       main = titolo_corrente, 
       ylim = c(-45, 5))
  
  # Aggiungiamo la linea orizzontale rossa
  abline(h = -2, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
}

# 3. Ripristiniamo la visualizzazione singola
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))


# 1. Prepariamo la griglia 2x2
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
# mar = c(bottom, left, top, right)
par(mar = c(4.1, 4.1, 3.1, 2.1))


# 2. Iteriamo attraverso i 4 elementi della lista
for (i in 1:4) {
  
  # Estraiamo i dati correnti per chiarezza
  dati_correnti <- list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$robust_z[1:100]
  titolo_corrente <- list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$plotTitle
  
  # Creiamo il plot seguendo le tue specifiche
  plot(dati_correnti, 
       xlab = "Genes", 
       ylab = "scores", 
       main = titolo_corrente, 
       ylim = c(-45, 5))
  
  # Aggiungiamo la linea orizzontale rossa
  abline(h = -2, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)
}

# 3. Ripristiniamo la visualizzazione singola
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

#########################################

#1. Prepariamo la griglia 2x2
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
# mar = c(bottom, left, top, right)
par(mar = c(4.1, 4.1, 3.1, 2.1))

new_titles = paste0(chosen_percentage, "% edges; ",
                    sign_proportions_specifier, ":", 100 - sign_proportions_specifier, 
                    " sign proportion") 

# 2. Iteriamo attraverso i 4 elementi della lista
for (i in 1:4) {
  
  # Estraiamo i dati correnti per chiarezza
  dati_correnti <- list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$p_values_adjusted
  titolo_corrente <- list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$plotTitle
  titolo_corrente = new_titles[i]
  
  
  # Creiamo il plot seguendo le tue specifiche
  plot(dati_correnti,
       log = "y",
       xlab = "Genes", 
       ylab = "q-value",
       pch = 19,
       col = "black",
       main = titolo_corrente,  
       ylim = c(0.001, 1))
  
  # # Aggiungiamo la linea orizzontale rossa
  abline(h = 0.05, col = "red", lwd = 3, lty = 2)
  abline(h = 10^(-3:0), col = "lightgray", lty = "solid")

  # Se vuoi le linee per i multipli (2, 3, 4...) tra una decade e l'altra:
  minor_ticks <- rep(1:9, 5) * 10^rep(-3:-1, each = 9)
  abline(h = minor_ticks, col = "lightgray", lwd = 0.1, lty = "solid")
}



# Creiamo una lista per memorizzare i 4 oggetti grafici
plot_list <- list()

for (i in 1:4) {
  
  # 1. Preparazione dati (come nel tuo codice)
  dati_correnti <- data.frame(
    ShapiroWilk = list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$resultShapiroWilk_first100,
    pValues = list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$p_values_raw < 0.05,
    qValues = list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$p_values_adjusted < 0.05,
    robustZ = (list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$robust_z < -2)[1:100]
  )
  
  # 2. Trasposizione per lo sviluppo ORIZZONTALE
  # t() trasforma il DF 100x3 in una Matrice 3x100
  matrice_plot <- t(as.matrix(dati_correnti) * 1)
  
  titolo_corrente <- list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$plotTitle
  
  # 3. Creazione del plot (senza visualizzarlo subito)
  # Usiamo silent = TRUE per non stampare singolarmente e salviamo il gtable
  p <- pheatmap(matrice_plot, 
                cluster_rows = FALSE,      
                cluster_cols = FALSE,      
                color = c("black", "white"), 
                border_color = "grey60",   
                legend = FALSE,            
                fontsize_row = 8,          # Leggermente pi√Ļ grande per le 3 righe
                fontsize_col = 1,         
                main = titolo_corrente,
                cellwidth = 5,             # Ridotta per far stare 100 geni in orizzontale
                cellheight = 15,           
                silent = TRUE              # Fondamentale per grid.arrange
  )
  
  # Salviamo l'oggetto grafico nella nostra lista
  plot_list[[i]] <- p$gtable
}

# 4. Visualizziamo i 4 subplot in una colonna (4 righe, 1 colonna)
grid.arrange(grobs = plot_list, ncol = 1)

# 1. Prepariamo la griglia 2x2
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
# mar = c(bottom, left, top, right)
par(mar = c(4.1, 4.1, 3.1, 2.1))


# 2. Iteriamo attraverso i 4 elementi della lista
for (i in 1:4) {
  
  # Estraiamo i dati correnti per chiarezza
  dati_correnti <- list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$robust_z
  titolo_corrente <- list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy[[i]]$plotTitle
  
  # Creiamo il plot seguendo le tue specifiche
  hist(dati_correnti, 
       xlab = "Genes", 
       ylab = "scores", 
       main = titolo_corrente, 
       )
  

}

# 3. Ripristiniamo la visualizzazione singola
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))


```

### Other Graphs
```{r, dpi=100, fig.width=5, fig.height=4, out.width="100%"}
chosen_percentage = "5"
list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy_5 = readRDS(paste0(mainFolder, 
                   "/list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy_", 
                   chosen_percentage,  "%"))

chosen_percentage = "10"
list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy_10 = readRDS(paste0(mainFolder, 
                   "/list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy_", 
                   chosen_percentage,  "%"))

# Combine the two main study lists into one single object containing 8 elements
combined_study_list <- c(list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy_5, 
                         list_of_statisticalEvaluation_onLBIStudy_10)

# Extract the 'external_gene_name' vector from the nested structure of each element
list_of_genes <- lapply(combined_study_list, function(element) {
  return(element$geneName_allIndexExclusive_first100$external_gene_name)
})

# Assign descriptive names to the list elements for better heatmap labeling
# (Assuming 4 elements for 5% and 4 elements for 10% study)
names(list_of_genes) <- c(paste0("5% ", sign_proportions_specifier, "(+)"), 
                          paste0("10% ", sign_proportions_specifier, "(+)"))

# Initialize an empty 8x8 matrix with zeros
num_elements <- length(list_of_genes)
common_gene_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = num_elements, ncol = num_elements)
common_gene_matrix_normalized = matrix(0, nrow = num_elements, ncol = num_elements)

# Set row and column names for the matrix based on the list names
rownames(common_gene_matrix) <- names(list_of_genes)
colnames(common_gene_matrix) <- names(list_of_genes)

# Fill the matrix by calculating the size of the intersection for each pair
for (i in 1:num_elements) {
  for (j in 1:num_elements) {
    # intersect() finds common strings, length() counts them
    common_gene_matrix[i, j] <- length(intersect(list_of_genes[[i]], 
                                                 list_of_genes[[j]]))
    common_gene_matrix_normalized[i, j] = 
      length(intersect(list_of_genes[[i]], list_of_genes[[j]]))/min(length(list_of_genes[[i]]),length(list_of_genes[[j]]))
  }
}



# Generate the heatmap visualization
pheatmap(common_gene_matrix, 
         display_numbers = TRUE,       # Show the actual count in each cell
         number_format = "%.0f",       # Display counts as integers
         cluster_rows = FALSE,          # Group similar studies together
         cluster_cols = FALSE, 
         color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "orange", "red"))(50),
         main = "Pairwise Gene Overlap Counts Between Configurations Couples",
         angle_col = 45)


rownames(common_gene_matrix_normalized) <- names(list_of_genes)
colnames(common_gene_matrix_normalized) <- names(list_of_genes)
common_gene_matrix_normalized_withNA = common_gene_matrix_normalized
# common_gene_matrix_normalized_withNA[lower.tri(common_gene_matrix_normalized, diag = TRUE)] <- NA
diag(common_gene_matrix_normalized_withNA) = NA

labels_matrix <- matrix("", nrow = nrow(common_gene_matrix_normalized_withNA), ncol = ncol(common_gene_matrix_normalized_withNA))
for (i in 1:nrow(common_gene_matrix_normalized_withNA)) {
  for (j in 1:ncol(common_gene_matrix_normalized_withNA)) {
    if (!is.na(common_gene_matrix_normalized_withNA[i, j])) {
      # Formattiamo il numero (es. 2 decimali)
      labels_matrix[i, j] <- sprintf("%.2f", common_gene_matrix_normalized_withNA[i, j])
    }
  }
}

# Generate the heatmap visualization (normalized version)
pheatmap(common_gene_matrix_normalized_withNA, 
         display_numbers = labels_matrix,       # Show the actual count in each cell
         cluster_rows = FALSE,          # Group similar studies together
         cluster_cols = FALSE, 
         color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "orange", "red"))(50),
         
         na_col = "grey10",              # Colora i NA di nero
         number_format = "%.2f",        # Formato per i numeri (es. 2 decimali)
         
         main = "Pairwise Gene Overlap Fractions Between Configurations Cuples",
         angle_col = 45)


# 1. Flatten the list of lists into a single vector of all genes found
# We use unique() inside lapply to ensure each gene is counted only once per list
unique_genes_per_list <- lapply(list_of_genes, unique)
all_genes_combined <- unlist(unique_genes_per_list)

# 2. Create a frequency table (Frequency of each gene across the 8 lists)
gene_frequency_counts <- table(all_genes_combined)

# 3. Convert the table to a data frame for easier manipulation
gene_frequency_df <- as.data.frame(gene_frequency_counts)
colnames(gene_frequency_df) <- c("Gene_Name", "Occurrence_Count")

# 4. Sort the data frame by frequency in descending order
gene_frequency_sorted <- gene_frequency_df[order(-gene_frequency_df$Occurrence_Count), ]

# 1. Select the Top 25 most frequent genes
num_ofTopGenes_identified = 50
topN_genes_identified <- head(gene_frequency_sorted, num_ofTopGenes_identified)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
# 2. Create the Bar Plot
# Adjust margins to fit long gene names if necessary
par(mar = c(8, 4, 4, 2)) 

barplot_heights <- topN_genes_identified$Occurrence_Count
gene_labels <- topN_genes_identified$Gene_Name

barplot_title = paste0("Top ", num_ofTopGenes_identified, 
                       " Most Frequent Genes Across Configurations")
# Draw the barplot
bp <- barplot(barplot_heights, 
              main = barplot_title,
              ylab = "Number of Lists (out of 8)",
              col = "steelblue",
              names.arg = "", # We will add labels manually for better control
              ylim = c(0, 8))

# Add the gene names on the x-axis with 45-degree rotation
text(x = bp, 
     y = -0.2, 
     labels = gene_labels, 
     srt = 45, 
     adj = 1, 
     xpd = TRUE, 
     cex = 0.8)

# Add a horizontal grid for better readability
abline(h = 1:8, col = "gray", lty = "dotted")


# Draw the barplot
bp <- barplot(gene_frequency_sorted$Occurrence_Count, 
              main = "Genes across studies",
              ylab = "Number of Lists (out of 8)",
              col = "steelblue",
              names.arg = "", # We will add labels manually for better control
              ylim = c(0, 8))

# Add the gene names on the x-axis with 45-degree rotation
text(x = bp, 
     y = -0.2, 
     labels = gene_frequency_sorted$Gene_Name, 
     srt = 45, 
     adj = 1, 
     xpd = TRUE, 
     cex = 0.3)

# Add a horizontal grid for better readability
abline(h = 1:8, col = "gray", lty = "dotted")




```
